summary: The diff introduces two unconditional runtime panics in the executable path: `main` divides by zero, and the new slice processing loop indexes one past the end of the slice. No builds or tests were run per instruction.

findings.title: Program now always panics by dividing by zero
findings.body: `main` changed the call from `divide(10, 2)` to `divide(10, 0)`. Since `divide` directly evaluates `a / b`, this causes an immediate integer divide-by-zero panic at runtime and prevents the program from reaching the subsequent processing call.

findings.title: Loop condition indexes one past the slice bounds
findings.body: The new `process` loop uses `i <= len(values)`, so the final iteration executes with `i == len(values)` and `values[i]` is out of range. This will panic for any input slice, including the `[]int{1, 2, 3}` passed from `main`; the condition should stop before `len(values)`.
